transliteration: śreyo hi jñānam abhyāsāj jñānād dhyānaṁ viśiṣyate | dhyānāt karma-phala-tyāgas tyāgāc chāntir anantaram ||
literal_meaning: Better indeed is knowledge through practice; better than knowledge is meditation; better than meditation is renunciation of the fruits of action; from renunciation peace follows immediately.
contextual_meaning: Krishna offers Arjuna a graded staircase of mind-training: start with disciplined study, deepen into meditative absorption, then surrender the need for outcomes; each step dissolves ego-clinging and moves the nervous system from threat to trust.
psychological_state: A client who feels they ‘should’ be able to leap straight to enlightenment but keeps tripping over daily anxieties; the verse normalizes incremental growth and gives permission to begin wherever they are.
trigger_scenario: A burnt-out college student scrolling self-help apps, convinced they must pick the ‘perfect’ productivity hack; instead they’re invited to: 1) study one concept deeply, 2) sit with it in daily mindfulness, 3) release the grade/GPA outcome, 4) notice the calm that arrives when the inner critic loosens its grip.
anchor_text: Study, meditate, then let go of results—peace follows.
